abort.no_devices_found: No Boks found. Make sure a Bluetooth adapter or an ESPHome Bluetooth proxy is in range, and that the official dongle is unplugged (it holds the connection).

init.title: Link settings
init.description: Saving reloads the link without restarting Home Assistant. Note: the integration's Python code is not reloaded — after updating the component files, a restart is still required.

data_description.keepalive: The Boks closes the connection after about 30 s of silence. A longer interval means fewer exchanges (and lower power draw) while the link is held, but moves closer to that cutoff: beyond it the link drops and reconnects in a loop, which costs more.
data_description.reconnect_max: Ceiling for the delay, which doubles on every failure. A high value spares the batteries while the Boks is out of range; a low one shortens the time to come back online.
data_description.open_code: 6 characters over the alphabet 0123456789AB. Filling this in adds an Open button: anyone with access to Home Assistant will then be able to open the mailbox. Leaving it empty keeps the integration strictly read-only. Use a permanent code (a master or fixed code from your account) — a single-use code would work exactly once.

error.invalid_open_code: Invalid code: 6 characters, only 0-9, A and B.
